name: Singapore Curry Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour into a bowl. Make a well in the center and add the egg.
2. Gradually stir in the milk and beat well until smooth. Stir in the cold water and beat well.
3. Pour the batter into a pitcher.
4. Heat a little oil in a small skillet. Pour off excess oil.
5. Pour a little batter into the skillet, swirling to spread evenly over the bottom to make a thin coating. Fry until the underside is golden.
6. Turn the pancake out of the pan (do not cook the other side). Repeat with remaining batter, adding more oil as needed, to make 8 pancakes.
7. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a saucepan. Add onion, ground beef, carrots, parsnip, and curry powder. Cook gently for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
8. Add tomato paste and mix well. Blend cornstarch with a little stock. Add the remaining stock to the meat mixture and bring to a boil.
9. Add the cornstarch mixture and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Simmer for 10 minutes.
10. Lay pancakes on a flat surface with cooked sides up. Spread filling in horizontal lines across the center, within 1-1/2 inches of the side edges.
11. Fold the side edges over the filling, then fold the remaining top and bottom edges to cover. Brush with egg.
12. Fold the pancakes in half. Chill for 1 hour.
13. Heat oil in a deep fat fryer or saucepan to 350°F (175°C). Fry folded pancakes, 4 at a time, for 2-3 minutes or until golden brown and heated through.
14. Drain on paper towels. Garnish with carrot strips if desired, and serve hot.
